title: heracleum sphondylium var. turugisanense
intro: Heracleum sphondylium var. turugisanense, also known as the wild angelica, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is a variant of Heracleum sphondylium. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of wild angelica include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of wild angelica often involve the roots or leaves being dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
